claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 21 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 21, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 21, which is 20 or over; Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education; Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right
circumstances: Child is 21, which is 20 or over
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, could you please tell me how many children you would like to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I can claim Child Benefit. I have one child - Alex, who is 21 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information about Alex.

Since Alex is 21 years old, I need to let you know that Child Benefit is normally only available for children under 16, or for those aged 16-19 who remain in approved education or training.

Unfortunately, at 21 years old, Alex is above the age limit for Child Benefit eligibility. The benefit can only continue up to age 20 in certain circumstances (if the child stays in approved education or training), but it cannot be claimed for anyone aged 20 or over.

Let me confirm this decision for you:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on my assessment, I'm sorry to say that **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

**Reason:** Alex is 21 years old, which is above the age limit for Child Benefit. The benefit is normally available for:
- Children under 16, or
- Young people aged 16-19 who stay in approved education or training (and in some cases can continue until they turn 20)

Since Alex is 21, they are too old to qualify for Child Benefit under any circumstances.

If you have any other questions or if there's anything else I can help you with, please let me know.

reasonings: Alex is 21 years old. Child Benefit is normally available for children under 16, or can continue for those aged 16-19 who remain in approved education or training. In some cases, it can extend until the child turns 20 if they stay in approved education or training. However, at 21 years old, Alex exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility. There is no provision under the Child Benefit rules to claim for a child aged 21 or over, regardless of their education or training status.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex because Alex is 21 years old, which exceeds the maximum age limit of under 20 (for those in approved education/training) or under 16 (general eligibility). Child Benefit cannot be claimed for anyone aged 20 or over.

In some cases, it can extend until the child turns 20 if they stay in approved education or training. However, at 21 years old, Alex exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility. There is no provision under the Child Benefit rules to claim for a child aged 21 or over, regardless of their education or training status.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex because Alex is 21 years old, which exceeds the maximum age limit of under 20 (for those in approved education/training) or under 16 (general eligibility). Child Benefit cannot be claimed for anyone aged 20 or over.
